input.background: A study of n=16 retains H0: μ=170 at an α of 0.05 (two sided). Based on prior study, the standard deviation of this variable is 40.
input.question: What was the power of the test to identify a population mean of 190?

output.answer: 1-β=Φ(-z_{1-α/2} + |μ0-μa|√n/σ) = Φ(-1.96 + |170-190|√16/40) = Φ(0.04) = 0.5160 [From Table B]. The test had about a 50/50 chance of rejecting the H0 under the stated conditions. This is low. Hypothesis tests should strive for at least 80% power.
